name: state-reset
description: After generating an initial equilibrium state, it is common to reset displacements, velocities, and histories before starting the construction or excavation stage.

common_patterns.name: Elastic initialization then reset displacements (FLAC2D)
notes: FLAC2D requires 'model large-strain on' (or off) before any solve / cycle command.
Displacement and velocity are nodal vector quantities → use 'zone gridpoint initialize' (NOT 'zone initialize').
FLAC2D vector tuple is (x,y); FLAC3D is (x,y,z).

common_patterns.name: Preserve stress through cmodel change
commands: zone cmodel assign mohr-coulomb range group 'stage'
zone property friction 35 cohesion 1e5 range group 'stage'
notes: Changing from one non-null constitutive model to another preserves zone stresses.
Assigning null removes stresses in affected zones.
Reset only the state variables that are artificial products of initialization; do not reset physical stress or pore-pressure state unless intended.
Save an initial-equilibrium state before staged model changes.
